registerdb:Block.registerdb:Name: SDS_MAC_AUX
registerdb:Block.registerdb:Short_Description: Network Interface Port: Configuration of MAC and PCS for 2 lanes, and LED interface.
registerdb:Block.registerdb:Full_Description: Network Interface Port: Configuration of MAC and PCS for 2 lanes, and LED interface. eth_0 : 0 eth_1 : 1

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: Indirect register write data or read data

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: IND_CMD
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: bit[0:7] is OP,bit[1] indicate read enable ,bit[2] indicate write enable. bit[8:15] is GROUP, bit[16；23] is INSTANCE bit[24;31] is ID bit[32:63] is ADDR

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description:
0x1 - Indirect register read. 0x2 - Indirect register write.
0x3~0x7 reserved

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: IND_CMD_RANGE
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Indirect Command Range: The SRAM end-line address for SRAM write/modify range.

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: Memory End Address: The SRAM end-line address for SRAM write/modify range

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: IND_CMD_BITMAP
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Indirect Command Range: The SRAM end-line address for SRAM write/modify range.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: IND_SMPH
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Indirect Command Range: The SRAM end-line address for SRAM write/modify range.

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: Owner ID. 0:semaphore is free Others:semaphore is busy

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: IND_STS
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Indirect Status: Indicates the status of the IND_CMD
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: Indirect Status: Indicates the status of the IND_CMD

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: Ready: Indicates that the command completed and a new command can be generated. 0 - Busy. 1 - Done. This bit is updated on every write (register IND_CMD) to the command register.
Indicates if the last command succeeded or failed. 0 - Error. 1 - Success. SUCCESS bit should be referred only when bit RDY = 1.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: IND_TIME_TH
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Indirect Command Range: The SRAM end-line address for SRAM write/modify range.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: BLOCK_ID
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Block Identity: Block and sub-block ID number for internal debugging purposes

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: link status indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port0
link status indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port1
RX FIFO overflow indication.0-no interrupt 1 - interrupt each bit per port0
RX FIFO overflow indication.0-no interrupt 1 - interrupt each bit per port1

MAC core corruption indication of RX side.
Indicate an unrecoverable error of MAC core, transmission is
suspended. Should Reset MAC core to recover.
User should Reset MAC core to recover if:
1. FIFO overflow/underflow automatic mode is not set.
2. MAC state machine is corrupt.0-no interrupt 1 - interrupt each bit per port0
TX FIFO underflow indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port0
TX FIFO underflow indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port1
TX FIFO overflow indication.0-no interrupt 1 - interrupt each bit per port0
TX FIFO overflow indication.0-no interrupt 1 - interrupt each bit per port1

MAC core corruption indication of TX side.
Indicate an unrecoverable error of MAC core, transmission is
suspended. Should Reset MAC core to recover.
User should Reset MAC core to recover if:
1. FIFO overflow/underflow automatic mode is not set.
2. MAC state machine is corrupt.0-no interrupt 1 - interrupt each bit per port0
MAC core corruption indication of TX side.port1
link down status indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port0
link down status indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port1
adapt TX FIFO0 overflow indication.0-no interrupt 1 - interrupt each bit per port0
adapt TX FIFO1 overflow indication.0-no interrupt 1 - interrupt each bit per port0

the field is the same as netif_int_src_reg_0
0 – no mask interrupt 1 – mask interrupt each bit per interrupt mask

MAC core corruption indication of RX side.
Indicate an unrecoverable error of MAC core, transmission is
suspended. Should Reset MAC core to recover.
User should Reset MAC core to recover if:
1. FIFO overflow/underflow automatic mode is not set.
2. MAC state machine is corrupt. = 1 - enable each bit per port0

MAC core corruption indication of TX side.
Indicate an unrecoverable error of MAC core, transmission is
suspended. Should Reset MAC core to recover.
User should Reset MAC core to recover if:
1. FIFO overflow/underflow automatic mode is not set.
2. MAC state machine is corrupt.=1 - enable each bit per port0

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_ADDR_ERROR_SELECT
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC split number and ECC ID

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: ECC ID Write Data: ECC ID of the SRAM

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_ADDR_ERROR_DATA_LOW
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Address Error Data Low: Provides the last ECC error address and type.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_ADDR_ERROR_DATA_HIGH
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Address Error Data High: Provides ECC error additional information

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: ECC syndrome (error bit according to the Hamming code).
ECC split number (ECC Split) (ECC may cover sections of an SRAM line).
Single Double Bit. 0 - Single bit error. 1 - Double bit error.
1 - The data in registers ECC_ADDR_ERROR_DATA_LOW and ECC_ADDR_ERROR_DATA_HIGH have been updated from the SRAM selected by register ECC_ADDR_ERROR_SELECT.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: SERR_INJ
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: SRAM ECC ID and ECC field index to inject the error.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: SRAM ECC ID and ECC field index to inject the error.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: DERR_INJ
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: SRAM ECC ID and ECC field index to inject the error.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: SRAM ECC ID and ECC field index to inject the error.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_ID_FIRST_LAST_SERR
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: first and last ECC ID with a error

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: Indicates first ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID) with a single error.
Indicates last ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID) with a single error.
1 - More than one ECC error occurred on the first ECC ID SRAM.
1 - More than one ECC error occurred on the last ECC ID SRAM.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_ID_FIRST_LAST_DERR
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: first and last ECC ID with a error

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: Indicates first ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID) with a double error.
Indicates last ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID) with a double error.
1 - More than one ECC error occurred on the first ECC ID SRAM.
1 - More than one ECC error occurred on the last ECC ID SRAM.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_SINGLE_ERROR_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Single Error 0: Provides a host interrupt cause for all SRAMs that have ECC or parity

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_SINGLE_ERROR_MASK_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Single Error mask 0: Provides a host interrupt cause for all SRAMs that have ECC or parity

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_DOUBLE_ERROR_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC double Error 0: Provides a host interrupt cause for all SRAMs that have ECC or parity

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_DOUBLE_ERROR_MASK_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC double Error mask 0: Provides a host interrupt cause for all SRAMs that have ECC or parity

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_DISABLE_FIX_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Disable Fix: Disables ECC calculation and fix that corrects single-error in SRAMs that have ECC protection

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_DISABLE_STATUS_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Disable Status 0: Disables SRAM ECC status indication.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_LATCH_MODE_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Latch Mode 0: Indicates if the first or last ECC error will be stored by the SRAM.

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: FULL DYNAMIC 0 - Disable 1 - Enable; bit[0]:port0 bit[1]:port1
FULL DYNAMIC 0 - Disable 1 - Enable; bit[0]:port0 bit[1]:port1

STATIC If bit[8] is set to 1, 10GR1 channel 0 for AUX MAC IP is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled If bit[9] is set to 1, 10GR1 channel 1 for AUX MAC IP is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled
FULL DYNAMIC 1-flow control enable for TX 0-flow control disable for TX bit[12]: port0 bit[13]: port1
STATIC 1-flow control pause enable for TX 0-flow control pfc enable for TX bit[14]: port0 bit[15]: port1
FULL DYNAMIC if bit[29] is set 1,when ip linkdown overtime tx drop to the chaneel0 if bit[30] is set 1,when ip linkdown overtime tx drop to the chaneel1
TX DROP time th： if TX DROP time > th,will drop pkt

RS algorithm configurations to per channel of CEPCS, should be consistent to the setting of CEFEC core（Fec has a total of 4 cores, and each core has 4 lanes that can process 200G.）. In current CEPCS version, only three different RS algorithms can be configured. To change value of these signals, should reset this channel to clean all interim results, and set new value during reset of this channel. For k = 0 to 3: If fec_mode[2k-1:2k] = 2’b00, this channel is configured to not use any RSFEC codec. If fec_mode [2k-1:2k] = 2’b01, This channel is configured to use RS(544,514) algorithm. If fec_mode [2k-1:2k] = 2’b10, This channel is configured to use RS(528,514) algorithm. If fec_mode [2k-1:2k] = 2’b11, This channel is configured to use RS(272,257) algorithm
When the pkt length is less than this value for port 0, error = 1
When the pkt length is less than this value for port 1, error = 1

STATIC Frame control for 10/25G /100Gframe for channel0 This is a control bus for 10/25G/100G frame If bit[0] is set to 1, FCS field should be generated and added to transmitted packet, otherwise, user logic should generate CRC for packet. Bit[1] is reserved, should be 0 always If bit[2] is set to 1, source address got from user logic should be replaced by the value in MAC REG, and bit[0] or bit[1] should also be asserted.

STATIC Frame control for 10/25G/100G frame for channel1 This is a control bus for 10/25G /100Gframe If bit[3] is set to 1, FCS field should be generated and added to transmitted packet, otherwise, user logic should generate CRC for packet. Bit[4] is reserved, should be 0 always If bit[5] is set to 1, source address got from user logic should be replaced by the value in MAC REG, and bit[0] or bit[1] should also be asserted.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CFG_FC_TX_CNT
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: flexe tx flow control configuration for channel0~79.

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: use as pfc and pause quanta

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CEPCS_STATUS
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Indications the pcs status for all 2 channels

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: Indications to link status for all 8 x channels, one bit per channel, bit[0] is used for channel 0. PCS Channels work in normal if 1. bit[0]:channel0 bit[1]:channel1
the counter for the drop pkt from aux_wrapper to maci， Including rx_en disable, ultra-short packets with errors and fc pkt bit[14:0]:cnt bit[15]:overturn
the counter for the pkt add eop from aux_wrapper to maci bit[14:0]:cnt bit[15]:overturn

0 - no mask 1 - maskwhen it is 1, the rx_adapter can unaffect about link_status . this configration masks link status and, when it is 1, mask link status , or no mask link status. when mac ip is mac loopback , pcs loopback and only tx transmiting data, this reg need to relative bit configration 1, or aux output vaild is 0. According to serdes lane configration.
0 - mask disable 1 - mask enable bit0 - port0 other error bit1 - port0 crc error bit2 - port1 other error bit3 - port1 crc error
the wrapper channel 0 counter for the tx drop bit[30:0]:cnt bit[31]:overturn
the wrapper channel 1 counter for the TX drop bit[30:0]:cnt bit[31]:overturn
the egress sop counter for aux mac wrapper input data bit[30:0]:cnt bit[31] :overturn
the egress eop counter for aux mac wrapper input data bit[30:0]:cnt bit[31] :overturn
the ingress sop counter for aux mac wrapper output data bit[30:0]:cnt bit[31] :overturn
the ingress eop counter for aux mac wrapper output data bit[30:0]:cnt bit[31] :overturn

Offset of the first bit of the current NET_IF_PORT block of 128 bits to be driven in the calendar. For example, in order to drive bits 10-19 of first NET_IF_PORT block in the pipe, configure the following: Register LED_CFG_START field CURRENT = 0. Register LED_CFG_END field CURRENT = 19. Register LED_CFG_START field OFFSET = 10.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: LED_CFG_START_CURRENT_REG
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: start bit of the current AUX

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: The first bit in the calendar of 4096 bits that is mapped to the current NET_IF_PORT block of 128 bits. The NET_IF_PORT that is the first in the pipe (master) must start at 0.
The last bit in the calendar of 4096 bits that is mapped to the current NET_IF_PORT block of 128 bits.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: LED_CFG_END_GLOBAL_REG
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: end bit of the global AUX
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: end bit of the global AUX

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: The last used bit of the calendar of 4096 bits. This value must be equal to the value of the LED_CFG_END CURRENT field of the last NET_IF_PORT block in the pipe. Valid only for the first NET_IF_PORT in the pipe (master).

LED clock frequency selector: Controls the frequency on pin LED_CLK. Use the same value for all NET_IF_PORT blocks. 0x0 - Core clock frequency / 512. 0x1 - Core clock frequency / 256. 0x2 - Core clock frequency / 128. 0x3 - Core clock frequency / 64. 0x4 - Core clock frequency / 32. 0x5 - Core clock frequency / 16.

Pulse width for dynamic signals (makes the signals visible). Use the same value for all NET_IF_PORT blocks. 0x0 - 2^24 system clock cycles. 0x1 - 2^23 system clock cycles. 0x2 - 2^22 system clock cycles. 0x3 - 2^21 system clock cycles. 0x4 - 2^20 system clock cycles. 0x5 - 2^19 system clock cycles. 0x6 - 2^18 system clock cycles. 0x7 - 2^9 system clock cycles. 0x8 - 2^7 system clock cycles.
Enable: 0 - LED interface is disabled. 1 - LED interface is enabled, and the LED bitmap of 128 bits has valid values.

Master Instance: 0 - This NET_IF_PORT block is not the first (master) in the pipe of NET_IF_PORT blocks. 1 - This NET_IF_PORT block is the first (master) in the pipe of NET_IF_PORT blocks. Only one of the 8 NET_IF_PORT blocks can be master.
User defined bits to be driven via the LED stream. For each bit i, case {LED_OVR_0[i], LED_OVER_1[i]}: 0x3 - On. 0x2 - Blink. 0x1/0x0 - Off.
User defined bits to be driven via the LED stream. For each bit i, case {LED_OVR_0[i], LED_OVER_1[i]}: 0x3 - On. 0x2 - Blink. 0x1/0x0 - Off.
When ipg wan enable, the length is for adapting the ipg slightly.The range of IPG is 7-24
When ipg wan enable, the length is for adapting the ipg slightly
Enable signal for IPG deletion. Due to the increased cost of AM insertion, IPG needs to be removed.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: REGFILE_CEMAC0_0_AINRANGELENGTHERRORS
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: stats for undersize pkt,modify by eco

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: When ipg wan enable, the length is for adapting the ipg slightly.The range of IPG is 7-24
When ipg wan enable, the length is for adapting the ipg slightly
Enable signal for IPG deletion. Due to the increased cost of AM insertion, IPG needs to be removed

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: REGFILE_CEMAC0_1_AINRANGELENGTHERRORS
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: stats for undersize pkt,modify by eco
